answers.answer: Use the login page with your username and password. If you do not have an account, open the register page and create one first.
Open the register page, enter username, password, and confirm password, then submit the form to create your OTT account.
The platform supports Basic, Standard, and Premium plans. You can open the Subscription Module from the dashboard to record a subscription.
Use the Payment button on the dashboard. It opens the mock payment page where you enter name, email, plan, and amount. Successful submissions are stored in the payment database.
Open Content Management from the dashboard to add titles, type, genre, description, and poster URL.
The Cookies Demo tracks your last visit and total number of visits using browser cookies.
Session Tracking shows current date and time along with the current session duration. A live session summary also appears on the dashboard.
Start Apache and MySQL in XAMPP, import the SQL file into the ott_platform database, and update db.php with the correct database credentials if required.
This chatbot works without internet because it reads answers from a local FAQ JSON file inside your OTT project.
This helpline bot can answer basic platform questions. If you need code changes or server fixes, check Tomcat logs and project files from the dashboard modules.
Only the admin user can access Content Management, Cookies Demo, and Session Tracking. Normal users can use the dashboard, chatbot, subscription, and payment modules.
The chatbot uses a local JSON knowledge file and matches your question with keywords. It is designed for offline support and basic platform guidance.
To run the JSP application, start Tomcat from the bin folder using startup.bat, then open the project URL on localhost:8080.
If shutdown.bat is disabled or the shutdown port is off, stop Tomcat with catalina.bat stop, by closing the terminal, or by stopping the Java process using the port.
On the dashboard, click a movie card to open the details view. It shows the poster, title, type, genre, and description in a focused modal.
The payment page supports UPI, Card, and Net Banking interfaces. Each method shows its own form section, but the backend still stores the transaction in the same payments table.
The UPI section includes a QR code image below the UPI ID field. It is part of the payment page layout and works as a visual scan-and-pay interface.
Use build-servlets.ps1 from the project root to compile Java servlet source files into WEB-INF/classes. Run it before testing servlet-based modules after source changes.
The payment module stores records in the MySQL database ott_platform, inside the payments table. You can inspect it through phpMyAdmin or with SQL queries.

fallback: I can answer basic OTT platform questions about login, registration, subscription, payment, content management, cookies, session tracking, and XAMPP setup.
